feed.title: The SEAL Lab
feed.subtitle: An engaging 1-3 sentence description of your lab.

entry.title: Lab members at ELM 4
entry.content:
Jiayi, Anzi, and Kexin (along with Eszter and Jiaming from the ExMean Group) attended the 4th Experiments in Linguistic Meaning (ELM) conference, which took place at the University of Pennsylvania from 6/10 to 12, 2026.

Eszter presented a talk (co-authored with Jiayi, Anzi, and Jiaming), titled “Evidence for Adaptation in Scalar Implicature Calculation”.

Kexin presented a talk (co-authored with Lance Rips), titled “Why isn’t ‘twenty-eight’ ‘ten ten eight’?”
entry.summary: Jiayi, Anzi, and Kexin (along with Eszter and Jiaming from the ExMean Group) attended the 4th Experiments in Linguistic Meaning (ELM) conference, which took place at the University of Pennsylvania from 6/10 to 12, 2026.

entry.title: Lab members at HSP 39
entry.content:
Jiayi, Anzi, and Shirley attended and presented at the 39th Human Sentence Processing (HSP) conference, which took place at MIT from 3/26 to 28, 2026.

Shirley presented her poster “Island Satiates without Change in Backgroundedness”. Anzi presented “Could 40% mean 10%? Adding an additional layer of uncertainty to the RSA framework”, work done in collaboration with Carolyn Anderson and Grusha Prasad.
entry.summary: Jiayi, Anzi, and Shirley attended and presented at the 39th Human Sentence Processing (HSP) conference, which took place at MIT from 3/26 to 28, 2026.
